era: 1981-present. Progressive Metal. Bellevue, Washington. EMI America. Key albums: Queensryche EP (1983), Rage for Order (1986), Operation: Mindcrime (1988), Empire (1990).

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST PROGRESSIVE METAL in engine. Prog metal = prog rock compositional complexity + heavy metal density. Concept albums, long songs, unusual time signatures, Geoff Tate operatic voice.
theory_nodes.description: Progressive metal enters the engine at degree 236.

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST BELLEVUE BAND (proper). Heart has Bellevue origins but is not primarily identified as Bellevue. Queensryche IS Bellevue's band. FIRST explicit east-side representative.
theory_nodes.description: Bellevue enters the engine at degree 236 with its defining band.

theory_nodes.concept: Operation: Mindcrime (1988) as concept album. 15 connected songs, one complete narrative. Nikki, Dr. X, Sister Mary. Political manipulation, moral failure, institutional betrayal. Universally ranked among rock's greatest concept albums.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 continues. Bellevue origins. Ann Wilson's operatic vocal and Geoff Tate's operatic voice are Pacific Northwest's two great trained rock vocalists.
Three consecutive degrees cover Bellevue's rock contribution comprehensively: Heart classic (234), Heart hard rock (235), Queensryche (236).

sps_pair.pairing_note: DOUBLE MILESTONE DEGREE. FIRST FISH + FIRST PROGRESSIVE METAL. E=5 salmon + E=7 Queensryche = E=12. Salmon semelparous sacrifice vs. Operation: Mindcrime's Nikki: both about individual purpose against systemic forces.

milestones: Double milestone degree with Pacific Salmon
